label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The text is about genetically modified (GM) foods and whether they can solve world hunger. The question specifically asks what GM companies promise farmers they will benefit from. Looking at the passage, there's a part that says: "While GM seeds are expensive, GM companies tell farmers that they will make good profits by saving money on pesticides." Then it contrasts that with data showing an increase in pesticide use. So the companies' promise is about saving money on pesticides, which relates to using fewer chemicals. The options are: A) practicing "green" farming B) use of less chemicals C) fair distribution of their crops D)
